title: silene psammitis subsp. psammitis
intro: Silene psammitis subsp. psammitis, also known as sand catchfly, is a plant species belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family and is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Its health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity. The herbal preparations of Silene psammitis subsp. psammitis include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including skin issues and infections.
